executive_summary: Total sales inc VAT for the week ending 16-May-2026 were 368,436.45, with sales ex VAT of 340,018.57 and an overall gross margin of 17.53% across the store.
The fresh departments P&L total reports sales of 111,314.47 ex VAT against a cost of sales of 91,501.02, delivering a blended fresh margin of 17.80% and a gross profit contribution of 19,813.45.

Total store waste of 8,111.35 represents 2.39% of sales ex VAT, which is an elevated figure and warrants immediate department-level investigation, particularly in Deli (1,253.18), Provisions (1,136.11), Produce (1,393.82), and Fish (862.65).

Scan margins are consistently running above reported P&L margins across all fresh departments, indicating unrecorded waste, supplier invoice variances, pricing errors, or stock count inaccuracies that are suppressing actual achieved margins.

Deli is the standout margin performer at 38.96% P&L margin (41.51% scan), generating 3,439.52 gross profit on 8,829.00 sales, though its waste figure of 1,253.18 is the second highest in the store and must be actively managed.

Pork and Bacon & Loose SRP are the two weakest fresh margin departments at 6.91% and 10.41% respectively, both significantly below scan margins of 20.65% and 11.62%, suggesting substantial unrecorded shrinkage or invoicing issues in Pork particularly.

Cigarettes returns zero profit and no sales, purchases, opening or closing stock data this week, which may indicate a reporting or system feed failure that needs to be investigated before next week's report.
biggest_wins: Deli: Strongest margin department in the store at 38.96% P&L margin, contributing 3,439.52 gross profit on sales of 8,829.00. Scan margin of 41.51% confirms underlying performance is solid.

Produce: Highest sales volume in the fresh P&L at 40,592.00, delivering 6,386.98 gross profit at a 15.73% margin. Largest single contributor to fresh profit this week despite elevated waste of 1,393.82.

Provisions: Strong sales of 25,937.00 with a margin of 17.76% generating 4,607.00 gross profit, broadly in line with scan margin of 19.71% and the second-highest profit contributor in the fresh block.
Beef: Solid performance with sales of 12,520.90, margin of 17.51%, and gross profit of 2,192.84. Scan margin of 20.51% versus P&L margin of 17.51% gap of 3 points is moderate and manageable.

Lamb: Achieved the highest fresh margin of 19.49% on sales of 1,591.76, generating 310.28 gross profit. Scan margin alignment at 24.47% is a gap to address but absolute profit contribution is acceptable for the category size.
biggest_risks: Pork: P&L margin of 6.91% against a scan margin of 20.65% is a gap of 13.74 percentage points, the largest margin variance in the store. Cost of sales of 1,591.35 against sales of 1,709.43 leaves only 118.08 gross profit. This gap almost certainly reflects unrecorded waste, markdown losses, or a supplier invoice discrepancy that must be investigated immediately.

Fish: Waste of 862.65 is disproportionately high relative to sales of 6,083.82, representing approximately 14.2% of fish sales lost to waste. P&L margin of 14.09% versus scan margin of 19.18% is a 5.09-point gap, further confirming significant unmanaged shrinkage in this department.

Deli: Despite strong margin, waste of 1,253.18 is the second highest absolute waste figure in the store. For a department with sales of 8,829.00, this waste represents approximately 14.2% of sales, which is unsustainable and will erode margin significantly if not addressed through better production planning and end-of-day markdowns.

Provisions: Waste of 1,136.11 on sales of 25,937.00 is the third highest waste figure. While the margin holds at 17.76%, tightening waste in this department would deliver meaningful profit improvement given the volume of sales flowing through it.

Produce: Waste of 1,393.82 is the highest in the store in absolute terms. On sales of 40,592.00 this equates to approximately 3.4% waste rate within the department, which is above acceptable thresholds for produce and suggests ordering quantities, rotation discipline, and markdown strategies need review.

Bacon & Loose SRP: P&L margin of 10.41% versus scan of 11.62% is a smaller gap but the absolute margin is low for a processed meat category. Cost of sales of 1,008.39 against sales of 1,125.56 leaves only 117.17 gross profit. Waste of 60.27 is adding further pressure.

Cigarettes: No sales, purchases, opening stock, closing stock, or cost of sales data is present for the week. If this is a system reporting failure, it means a typically high-volume category is entirely absent from the margin calculation, which will distort overall store margin reporting. Confirm with IT and store systems team urgently.

Scan vs P&L margin gap across all departments: Every department shows scan margin exceeding P&L margin, which is a systemic pattern pointing to one or more of the following: unrecorded waste not being posted, supplier invoices being processed at higher than shelf-cost values, or stocktake discrepancies. A review of waste posting compliance and invoice matching should be prioritised.
department_callouts: Beef: Sales 12,520.90, margin 17.51%, waste 84.07. Performing in line with expectations. Scan margin gap of 3 points (20.51% vs 17.51%) is moderate. Monitor waste and ensure all markdowns are being recorded.
Lamb: Sales 1,591.76, margin 19.49%, waste 93.54. Best P&L margin in meat block. Scan margin gap of 4.98 points warrants a check on waste recording and pricing accuracy.

Pork: Sales 1,709.43, margin 6.91%, waste 81.87. Critical underperformer. Scan-to-P&L gap of 13.74 points is the largest in the store. Requires immediate investigation into invoicing, waste posting, and pricing.
Bacon & Loose SRP: Sales 1,125.56, margin 10.41%, waste 60.27. Low margin category with a gap of 1.21 points to scan. Ensure all SRP lines are priced correctly and waste is fully posted.

Poultry: Sales 12,925.00, margin 13.80%, waste 202.38. Waste is elevated for poultry and the scan gap of 2.61 points suggests some shrinkage is not being captured. Review daily rotation and production volumes.

Fish: Sales 6,083.82, margin 14.09%, waste 862.65. Waste as a proportion of sales is approximately 14.2%, which is critically high. This department requires an immediate ordering and production review.

Produce: Sales 40,592.00, margin 15.73%, waste 1,393.82. Highest waste in absolute terms. Margin is acceptable but waste is eroding potential profitability. Ordering quantities, delivery schedules, and markdown disciplines should be reviewed.

Deli: Sales 8,829.00, margin 38.96%, waste 1,253.18. Excellent margin performance but waste at 14.2% of sales is unsustainable. Production planning and end-of-day markdown and donation procedures need tightening urgently.
Provisions: Sales 25,937.00, margin 17.76%, waste 1,136.11. Solid margin but waste is the third highest in the store. Short-dated line management and rotation compliance should be reviewed.

Cigarettes: No data available for the week. This is a reporting anomaly that must be resolved. Cigarettes typically represent a significant scan sales volume (12.37% scan share noted) and its absence from the P&L distorts overall margin reporting.
Grocery Impulse: Waste of 434.67 is the highest in the non-fresh categories. Review planogram compliance, date rotation on confectionery and snacking lines, and any promotional overstocking.
Bread and Cakes: Waste of 735.63 is significant for ambient bakery and in-store bakery combined. Review daily order quantities, time-of-day markdown procedures, and end-of-day surplus management.
Dairy: Waste of 379.40 should be reviewed against date profiles on key lines. Ensure rotation discipline is enforced across all chilled dairy bays.
Frozen: Waste of 128.18 in frozen is relatively low but should be monitored for temperature compliance incidents that may be driving unplanned write-offs.
action_plan: Pork department - immediate investigation required: Pull invoices for the week and cross-reference against goods-received notes to identify any over-invoicing. Check that all waste and markdown activity has been posted correctly. A 13.74-point gap between scan and P&L margin cannot be explained by normal trading variance alone. Report findings to category manager by Wednesday.

Fish department - waste reduction plan: Fish waste of 862.65 representing approximately 14.2% of department sales is at crisis level. Department manager to review daily order quantities against actual sales velocity for the previous four weeks. Implement a twice-daily date check and introduce a structured markdown schedule at 50% off for fish within one day of use-by date. Target waste reduction to below 7% of fish sales within two weeks.

Deli department - production planning review: Despite excellent margin, waste of 1,253.18 represents 14.2% of deli sales. Department manager to introduce daily production logs aligned to sales-by-hour data. End-of-day surplus must be marked down before close. Explore food donation partnership for unsold prepared foods. Target waste below 8% of deli sales.

Produce department - ordering and markdown audit: Waste of 1,393.82 is the highest absolute waste figure in the store. Store manager to conduct a full ordering review with the produce department manager, comparing order quantities to achieved sales for the top 20 SKUs. Markdown process for short-dated produce to be reviewed and enforced daily. Target waste reduction of 20% week-on-week.

Provisions department - rotation compliance check: Waste of 1,136.11 on a high-volume department requires a structured response. Team leader to conduct a full date rotation audit across all chilled and ambient provisions bays. All lines within five days of best-before to be front-faced and a markdown or transfer plan applied. Waste posting compliance to be verified.

Cigarettes reporting failure - IT and systems escalation: No financial data has been captured for Cigarettes this week despite a scan contribution of 12.37%. Store manager to contact SuperValu IT helpdesk and the reporting team on Monday morning to identify the cause of the missing data feed and ensure it is corrected before the week-16 report is finalised. Back-post data if possible.

Scan vs P&L margin gap audit - all fresh departments: Every fresh department shows scan margin above P&L margin this week. Store manager and fresh department managers to conduct a joint review of waste posting compliance. All waste must be posted on the day it occurs. Any backdated waste from prior periods should be identified and posted with correct date references to avoid further distortion.

Bread and cakes waste management: Waste of 735.63 across bakery lines requires a structured response. Bakery manager or responsible team leader to review daily bake-off quantities, introduce a PM markdown schedule no later than two hours before store close, and consider reducing fresh delivery frequencies on slower lines.

Dairy waste reduction: Waste of 379.40 should prompt a full date check of all chilled dairy bays. Team to identify the top five SKUs driving waste, review order quantities on those lines, and ensure FIFO rotation is being enforced at every delivery.

Grocery impulse waste review: Waste of 434.67 in grocery impulse is the highest in the ambient non-fresh block. Category manager to identify top waste SKUs, check planogram compliance, review promotional stock quantities, and ensure any near-date confectionery and snacking lines are being actioned promptly.

Poultry department - shrinkage and waste review: Waste of 202.38 combined with a 2.61-point scan-to-P&L margin gap suggests unrecorded shrinkage. Poultry manager to review daily production quantities, confirm all waste is being posted, and review portion accuracy on any counter or pre-pack lines.

Weekly waste meeting: Store manager to institute a standing 15-minute waste review meeting every Monday morning with all fresh and chilled department managers, using the prior week waste breakdown report as the agenda. Targets to be set per department and tracked week-on-week with accountability assigned at department manager level.
